title: Religio
abstract: This course develops Vervaeke's concept of religio — the deep, pre-institutional, transjective binding of agent and arena that underlies all individual acts of meaning-making and all religious and spiritual traditions. Students will be able to distinguish religio from both institutional religion and from its propositional counterpart credo, explain how religio functions as higher-order relevance realization, and articulate why the modern meaning crisis is partly a crisis of religio — the collapse of the lived participatory binding into mere creedal belief. In the series arc, religio is the concept that bridges cognitive science (relevance realization, agent-arena relationship) to the normative question of what wisdom practices must cultivate in order to address the perennial problems of human existence.

modules.title: Religio as Higher-Order Relevance Realization
modules.learningObjective: Explain what religio is as a cognitive-existential structure and why it is described as higher-order relevance realization rather than a feeling or institutional affiliation.

expositionPassages.phrase: We can use Mythos to activate, accelerate, articulate, and appreciate Religio. Religio is inherently valuable to us so even that alone is going to be very valuable to us.
expositionPassages.note: Religio as the target that mythos serves — not the other way around

expositionPassages.phrase: Religio is… I'm using it in a spiritual sense, [in] the sense of a pre-egoic, ultimately a post-egoic, binding that simultaneously grounds the self and its world.
expositionPassages.note: Definition: religio as the pre-egoic binding prior to any subject-object split

socraticSeeds.prompt: Have you ever had a moment when the boundary between yourself and what you were doing dissolved — total absorption in music, nature, a conversation? What was happening to the ordinary distinction between 'you' and 'it'?

Vervaeke describes religio as 'higher-order relevance realization.' What would it mean for relevance realization to operate on itself — for the whole process of meaning-making to become relevant to itself?
If religio is pre-egoic and 'simultaneously grounds the self and its world,' does it precede the self-world distinction altogether? What kind of experience might that be?
Vervaeke says religio is 'inherently valuable.' What would make a cognitive-existential structure valuable independently of any belief system built upon it?

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Religio is simply a feeling of the numinous or mystical awe.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Religio is a cognitive-existential structure — the higher-order relevance realization that grounds all individual meaning systems — not a feeling, though such feelings may be its phenomenal expression.

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Religio is religion — it is about belonging to or practicing within a religious tradition.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Religio is pre-institutional and pre-propositional; it is the participatory binding that religious institutions have historically tried to activate, but it exists independently of any particular tradition.

checkForUnderstanding.prompt: In what sense is religio the 'ground' of all individual meaning systems rather than one meaning system among others — and what follows for how we should think about recovering religio in response to the meaning crisis?
expectedThemes: religio prior to all specific meanings
practices must target religio not beliefs

modules.title: Religio versus Credo: The Collapse That Created the Meaning Crisis
modules.learningObjective: Articulate the distinction between religio and credo and explain how the historical collapse of religio into credo contributed to the modern meaning crisis.

expositionPassages.phrase: And so I think that is something that should be acknowledged as what we would be looking for. And this brings out an important contrast (writes = beside Religio) I want to discuss (erases equal sign). Sorry, not equal. Religio versus credo
expositionPassages.note: The pivotal distinction: religio is not equal to credo

expositionPassages.phrase: one of the things that wisdom is doing that's also really important is it's enhancing religio (writes Religio below Wisdom). That's a way in which you can powerfully—and we saw already the connection.
expositionPassages.note: Wisdom enhances religio — the practical upshot of recovering the distinction

socraticSeeds.prompt: Credo means creed — the propositional content of what one believes. Religio is the lived participatory binding. Can you imagine someone who sincerely assents to every doctrine of a religion but feels entirely empty, dead inside, disconnected from everything? What has that person lost?
Can religio exist without credo — a deep sense of binding and orientation without any particular doctrinal commitment? What would that look like?

Vervaeke argues that modernity has largely collapsed religio into credo — treating religion as primarily a matter of holding the right beliefs. What cultural, historical, or institutional forces might have produced this collapse?
If recovering religio does not require adopting a particular credo, what would it require? What practices, conditions, or transformations might reactivate the binding that religio names?

misconceptionBranches.misconception: The meaning crisis can be solved by returning to creedal religion — by people believing the right things again.
misconceptionBranches.correction: What is needed is recovering religio, not credo; the meaning crisis is a crisis of participatory binding, not of incorrect propositions, and it can be addressed through a 'religion that is not a religion' without metaphysical commitment.

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Vervaeke's distinction between religio and credo is an attack on religious belief.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Vervaeke explicitly respects the indispensability of particular creeds and myths for particular people; his critique targets the reduction of religion to credo divorced from the participatory practices that give creeds their existential power.

checkForUnderstanding.prompt: How does the collapse of religio into credo illuminate the modern predicament — and why does Vervaeke argue that the solution requires more than better arguments or more sophisticated doctrines?

modules.title: Religio as Pre-Egoic and Post-Egoic: The Ground That Precedes and Exceeds the Self
modules.learningObjective: Explain the sense in which religio is both pre-egoic and post-egoic, and why this double character makes it the target of wisdom traditions across cultures.

expositionPassages.phrase: Religio is… I'm using it in a spiritual sense, [in] the sense of a pre-egoic, ultimately a post-egoic, binding that simultaneously grounds the self and its world.
expositionPassages.note: The double character: religio precedes and transcends the ego while grounding both self and world

expositionPassages.phrase: We can use Mythos to activate, accelerate, articulate, and appreciate Religio. Religio is inherently valuable to us so even that alone is going to be very valuable to us.
expositionPassages.note: Mythos activates but does not constitute religio

socraticSeeds.prompt: Vervaeke says religio is both pre-egoic (prior to the formation of the self) and post-egoic (beyond ordinary ego-boundaries). What kind of experience might be described as both before the self and after the self?

If religio 'simultaneously grounds the self and its world,' it seems to be the condition that makes the self-world distinction possible at all. What would it feel like to become aware of that ground — to touch what normally just operates beneath awareness?

Wisdom traditions across cultures — Buddhist awakening, Neoplatonic henosis, Christian union with God — have historically aimed at a transformation that goes beyond the ordinary ego. Could religio be a cognitive-scientific way of naming what all these traditions converge on?

Vervaeke says mythos can 'activate, accelerate, articulate, and appreciate' religio — but cannot constitute it. What is the difference between activating something and constituting it? Why does this distinction matter for practice?

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Religio is a kind of spiritual self-absorption or navel-gazing — it is about the self.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Religio is precisely the dissolution of ordinary self-absorption: it is the pre-egoic and post-egoic binding in which the sharp boundary between self and world is suspended, not reinforced.

checkForUnderstanding.prompt: Why does Vervaeke argue that wisdom must target religio rather than merely producing correct beliefs or good behaviors — and what kind of transformation is required to access what religio names?
